001package guiStaffHome;
002
003import javafx.geometry.Pos;
004import javafx.scene.Scene;
005import javafx.scene.control.Button;
006import javafx.scene.control.Label;
007import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
008import javafx.scene.shape.Line;
009import javafx.scene.text.Font;
010import javafx.stage.Stage;
011import database.Database;
012//import database.Database;
013import entityClasses.User;
014
015
016/*******
017 * <p> Title: ViewStaffHome Class. </p>
018 * 
019 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based Role2 Home Page.  The page is a stub for some role needed for
020 * the application.  The widgets on this page are likely the minimum number and kind for other role
021 * pages that may be needed.</p>
022 * 
023 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
024 * 
025 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
026 * 
027 * @version 1.00                2025-04-20 Initial version
028 *  
029 */
030
031public class ViewStaffHome {
032        
033        /*-*******************************************************************************************
034
035        Attributes
036        
037         */
038        
039        // These are the application values required by the user interface
040        
041        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
042        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
043
044
045        // These are the widget attributes for the GUI. There are 3 areas for this GUI.
046        
047        // GUI Area 1: It informs the user about the purpose of this page, whose account is being used,
048        // and a button to allow this user to update the account settings
049        protected static Label label_PageTitle = new Label();
050        protected static Label label_UserDetails = new Label();
051        protected static Button button_UpdateThisUser = new Button("Account Update");
052                
053        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
054        protected static Line line_Separator1 = new Line(20, 95, width-20, 95);
055
056        // GUI ARea 2: This is a stub, so there are no widgets here.  For an actual role page, this are
057        // would contain the widgets needed for the user to play the assigned role.
058        protected static Button button_DiscussionBoard = new Button("Discussion Board");
059        protected static Button button_TeacherGradebook = new Button("Teacher Gradebook");
060        
061        
062        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
063        protected static Line line_Separator4 = new Line(20, 525, width-20,525);
064        
065        // GUI Area 3: This is last of the GUI areas.  It is used for quitting the application and for
066        // logging out.
067        protected static Button button_BackToLogin = new Button("Back to Login");
068        protected static Button button_Logout = new Button("Logout");
069        protected static Button button_Quit = new Button("Quit");
070
071        // This is the end of the GUI objects for the page.
072        
073        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
074        private static ViewStaffHome theView;           // Used to determine if instantiation of the class
075                                                                                                // is needed
076
077        // Reference for the in-memory database so this package has access
078        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;
079
080        protected static Stage theStage;                        // The Stage that JavaFX has established for us 
081        protected static Pane theRootPane;                      // The Pane that holds all the GUI widgets
082        protected static User theUser;                          // The current logged in User
083        
084        private static Scene theStaffHomeScene;         // The shared Scene each invocation populates
085        protected static final int theRole = 3;         // Admin: 1; Role1: 2; Role2: 3
086
087        /*-*******************************************************************************************
088
089        Constructors
090        
091         */
092
093        /**********
094         * <p> Method: displayStaffHome(Stage ps, User user) </p>
095         * 
096         * <p> Description: This method is the single entry point from outside this package to cause
097         * the Role2 Home page to be displayed.
098         * 
099         * It first sets up every shared attributes so we don't have to pass parameters.
100         * 
101         * It then checks to see if the page has been setup.  If not, it instantiates the class, 
102         * initializes all the static aspects of the GIUI widgets (e.g., location on the page, font,
103         * size, and any methods to be performed).
104         * 
105         * After the instantiation, the code then populates the elements that change based on the user
106         * and the system's current state.  It then sets the Scene onto the stage, and makes it visible
107         * to the user.
108         * 
109         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
110         * 
111         * @param user specifies the User for this GUI and it's methods
112         * 
113         */
114        public static void displayStaffHome(Stage ps, User user) {
115                
116                // Establish the references to the GUI and the current user
117                theStage = ps;
118                theUser = user;
119                
120                // If not yet established, populate the static aspects of the GUI
121                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewStaffHome();             // Instantiate singleton if needed
122                
123                // Populate the dynamic aspects of the GUI with the data from the user and the current
124                // state of the system.
125                theDatabase.getUserAccountDetails(user.getUserName());
126                applicationMain.FoundationsMain.activeHomePage = theRole;
127                
128                label_UserDetails.setText("User: " + theUser.getUserName());// Set the username
129
130                // Set the title for the window, display the page, and wait for the Admin to do something
131                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundations: Role2 Home Page");
132                theStage.setScene(theStaffHomeScene);                                           // Set this page onto the stage
133                theStage.show();                                                                                        // Display it to the user
134        }
135        
136        /**********
137         * <p> Method: ViewStaffHome() </p>
138         * 
139         * <p> Description: This method initializes all the elements of the graphical user interface.
140         * This method determines the location, size, font, color, and change and event handlers for
141         * each GUI object. </p>
142         * 
143         * This is a singleton and is only performed once.  Subsequent uses fill in the changeable
144         * fields using the displayStaffHome method.</p>
145         * 
146         */
147        private ViewStaffHome() {
148                
149                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
150                theRootPane = new Pane();
151                theStaffHomeScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);      // Create the scene
152                
153                // Set the title for the window
154                
155                // Populate the window with the title and other common widgets and set their static state
156                
157                // GUI Area 1
158                label_PageTitle.setText("Staff Home Page");
159                setupLabelUI(label_PageTitle, "Arial", 28, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 5);
160
161                label_UserDetails.setText("User: " + theUser.getUserName());
162                setupLabelUI(label_UserDetails, "Arial", 20, width, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 55);
163                
164                setupButtonUI(button_UpdateThisUser, "Dialog", 18, 170, Pos.CENTER, 610, 45);
165                button_UpdateThisUser.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerStaffHome.performUpdate(); });
166                
167                // GUI Area 2
168                setupButtonUI(button_DiscussionBoard, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 140);
169                button_DiscussionBoard.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerStaffHome.performOpenDiscussion(); });
170
171                setupButtonUI(button_TeacherGradebook, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 290, 140);
172                button_TeacherGradebook.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerStaffHome.performOpenTeacherGradebook(); });
173                
174                
175                // GUI Area 3
176        setupButtonUI(button_BackToLogin, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 580, 540);
177        button_BackToLogin.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerStaffHome.performBackToLogin(); });
178        
179        setupButtonUI(button_Logout, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 540);
180        button_Logout.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerStaffHome.performLogout(); });
181        
182        setupButtonUI(button_Quit, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 300, 540);
183        button_Quit.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerStaffHome.performQuit(); });
184
185                // This is the end of the GUI initialization code
186                
187                // Place all of the widget items into the Root Pane's list of children
188        theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(
189                        label_PageTitle, label_UserDetails, button_UpdateThisUser, line_Separator1,
190                        button_DiscussionBoard,
191                        button_TeacherGradebook,
192                line_Separator4, button_BackToLogin, button_Logout, button_Quit);
193        }
194        
195        
196        /*-********************************************************************************************
197
198        Helper methods to reduce code length
199
200         */
201        
202        /**********
203         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
204         * 
205         * @param l             The Label object to be initialized
206         * @param ff    The font to be used
207         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
208         * @param w             The width of the Button
209         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
210         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
211         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
212         */
213        private static void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, 
214                        double y){
215                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
216                l.setMinWidth(w);
217                l.setAlignment(p);
218                l.setLayoutX(x);
219                l.setLayoutY(y);                
220        }
221        
222        
223        /**********
224         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
225         * 
226         * @param b             The Button object to be initialized
227         * @param ff    The font to be used
228         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
229         * @param w             The width of the Button
230         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
231         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
232         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
233         */
234        private static void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, 
235                        double y){
236                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
237                b.setMinWidth(w);
238                b.setAlignment(p);
239                b.setLayoutX(x);
240                b.setLayoutY(y);                
241        }
242}
